name: Loin of Lamb with Apple and Ginger Stuffing (
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and core the apples, then slice thinly. Put them in a saucepan with the lemon juice, sugar, and ginger.
2. Cook over gentle heat until the apples are just soft. Set aside to cool.
3. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
4. Trim the lamb, remove the skin, and score the fat.
5. Lay the joint out on a board, fat side down.
6. Spoon the apple mixture along the center of the lamb.
7. Roll up and tie with twine.
8. Peel the garlic and cut it into slivers.
9. Pierce the joint all over with a sharp knife.
10. Slip the garlic slivers into the pockets formed in the lamb.
11. Season with salt and pepper.
12. Put the joint in a roasting pan and cook for 30 minutes in the hot oven.
13. Heat the cider in a small pan and pour it over the lamb.
14. Reduce the heat to moderate (350°F) and cook for another 40 minutes, basting frequently.
15. When the lamb is cooked, transfer it to a heated serving dish and keep warm.
16. Remove any excess fat from the pan juices, reduce slightly over high heat, and serve with sliced lamb.
